title: HUF Repository — GitHub Setup Handoff for ChatGPT
for: ChatGPT — to assist Peter Higgins with GitHub repo setup
classification: OPERATIONAL — repo deployment instructions. Not part of the HUF corpus. This is a tool for getting the corpus published.

context_for_chatgpt.who_is_peter: Peter Higgins, independent researcher, Rogue Wave Audio (RWA). Built HUF (Heterogeneous Uniform Framework) over several months with a collective of 5 AI systems (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Grok). HUF establishes composition monitoring as a fourth monitoring category alongside magnitude, identity, and trend.
context_for_chatgpt.what_is_huf: A domain-agnostic monitoring framework that reads the internal structural concentration of any proportional system. Built on 40+ years of established compositional data analysis (Aitchison 1982). The core innovation: applying Aitchison's simplex geometry as a primary monitoring observable, not just a statistical correction. Demonstrated across 8 domains including finance, electricity, GDP sectors, hard drives, and astrophysics.
context_for_chatgpt.why_now: Independent convergence: Dr. Jenny Wagner (astrophysicist, 30+ years) independently arrived at ratio primacy from gravitational lensing. The mathematical foundations keep confirming from multiple directions. Time to publish.
context_for_chatgpt.peter_uses: GitHub Desktop on Windows. Local paths: D:\GitHub\HUF and D:\GitHub\RWA. Short paths chosen deliberately for long scientific filenames.
context_for_chatgpt.what_peter_needs_from_you: Help organizing the 254-file corpus into the two repos, creating proper .gitignore, verifying structure, and pushing via GitHub Desktop. Peter will copy files from his working folder into the repo folders. You help him verify everything is right before the push.

repo_1.name: HUF
repo_1.description: HUF — Heterogeneous Uniform Framework. Composition monitoring as the fourth monitoring category.
repo_1.what_goes_here: The entire HUF-repo corpus: all 254 files, all subdirectories, preserving the exact structure documented below.

repo_2.name: RWA
repo_2.description: Rogue Wave Audio — corporate and outreach materials
repo_2.what_goes_here: Peterson letter, outreach strategy, corporate reference. NOT the HUF corpus. This is the business wrapper.
repo_2.note: This repo is separate from HUF. The Peterson letter references the HUF repo by URL.

root_files.description: Main repo README — describes what HUF is, how to navigate, and the triad architecture

huf-gov/.description: HUF-GOV: Observation Layer (SEES). The open-loop monitoring instrument.

subdirs.evidence/case-studies/backblaze/: Hard drive failure composition analysis (Backblaze dataset)
subdirs.evidence/case-studies/energy/: EMBER electricity fuel mix analysis (5 countries, 2000-2025)
subdirs.evidence/case-studies/gdp/: World Bank GDP sector composition analysis
subdirs.evidence/case-studies/ramsar/: Ramsar wetland species composition (synthetic test data)
subdirs.evidence/scale-invariance/: Scale invariance simulation demonstrating domain-agnostic property
subdirs.governance/: Safe operations doctrine: LOOP-001 (open loop), KILL-001 (19 failure modes), SAFE-001, HAGF-001
subdirs.science/: Core science: MONITOR-001 (taxonomy), PAPER-001, TRANS-001 (information bridge), ONTO-001 (ontology)
subdirs.tools/spectrum-analyzer/: HUF Spectrum Analyzer — v1 (JSX), v2 (HTML), v3 (JSX with phase display)

huf-cls/.description: HUF-CLS: Closed-Loop System (DOES). Published under MIT for transparency. Contains the actuator architecture.

subdirs.architecture/: GOV-001 architecture spec, CL01-CL05 control loop stack
subdirs.calibration/: CL03 sensitivity analysis, CL05 calibration study
subdirs.formulas/: DISP-001 phase display, SAI-001 amplifier, Grok integration filter
subdirs.routing/: Multi-AI routing assessments for each collective member

huf-cls/.governance_note: README.md in this directory contains explicit warning: read the kill test before closing the loop.

context-books/.description: Audience-specific plain-language explanations of HUF concepts

math-books/.description: Formal mathematical reference — the Aitchison geometry, ILR transforms, Fisher metric

drafts/.description: Work in progress — paper drafts for various venues, not yet submitted

subdirs.accord/: Human-AI Accord draft for Ramsar application
subdirs.papers/: Draft papers for JRSS-B, CoDa journal, CBD, GEOBON, Horizon Europe, IFAC/IEEE, NERC, Ramsar

process/.description: How HUF was built — the development process itself, documented for transparency

subdirs.governance-docs/: Voting register, decision log, deployment strategy, personal plan
subdirs.review-traces/: Complete review trace history (v1.0 through v5.10) — 26 documents showing every collective review cycle

process/.note: This directory is the provenance chain. It proves HUF was built with documented governance, not invented overnight.

reference/.description: Deep reference material — the technical backbone

subdirs.machine-readable/: 68 JSON documents — the complete machine-readable corpus. Every specification, analysis, assessment, and schema. This is the heart of the repo.
subdirs.pillars/: 17 pillar documents — the foundational analyses that support the framework
subdirs.technical-notes/: 18 technical notes — detailed explorations of specific topics
subdirs.wiki/: 4 wiki-format documents — entry points for different audiences

gitignore_content:
# OS files
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db
desktop.ini

# Editor files
*.swp
*.swo
*~
.vscode/
.idea/

# Temporary files
*.tmp
*.bak

# Node (if running analyzer locally)
node_modules/
package-lock.json

# Python
__pycache__/
*.pyc
.env

step_1_create_repos.instruction: In GitHub Desktop: File > New Repository. Name: HUF. Local path: D:\GitHub\HUF. Initialize with README: NO (we have our own). License: MIT (we have our own). Create.
step_1_create_repos.then: Do the same for RWA at D:\GitHub\RWA if not already created.

step_2_copy_files.instruction: Copy the ENTIRE contents of your HUF-repo working folder into D:\GitHub\HUF\. The structure should be D:\GitHub\HUF\LICENSE, D:\GitHub\HUF\README.md, D:\GitHub\HUF\huf-gov\, etc.
step_2_copy_files.verify: In GitHub Desktop, you should see 254 new files listed as changes. The directory structure should match the manifest above exactly.

step_3_add_gitignore.instruction: Create a file called .gitignore in D:\GitHub\HUF\ with the content from the gitignore_content field above.
step_3_add_gitignore.note: On Windows, you may need to create this via command line or text editor. Some file explorers won't let you create files starting with a dot.

step_4_publish.instruction: In GitHub Desktop: Repository > Publish repository. Name: HUF. Description: 'HUF - Heterogeneous Uniform Framework. Composition monitoring as the fourth monitoring category.' Keep it PUBLIC. Publish.
step_4_publish.note: This is the Bell moment. Once published, the timestamp is on the record.

step_5_verify.instruction: Go to github.com/PeterHiggins19/Higgins-Unity-Framework in your browser. Verify: README displays correctly, directory structure is visible, all 254 files are present, LICENSE shows MIT.
step_5_verify.smoke_test: Click into huf-gov/tools/spectrum-analyzer/ and verify the .jsx and .html files are there. Click into reference/machine-readable/ and verify the JSON files render properly.

rwa_repo_contents.note: The RWA repo is for Rogue Wave Audio corporate materials. Keep it separate from HUF.
rwa_repo_contents.note_2: The Peterson letter references the HUF repo URL. Make sure the HUF repo is live BEFORE sending the letter.

commit_message_suggestion:
Initial commit: HUF v1.0 — 254 files, complete corpus

Heterogeneous Uniform Framework (HUF)
Composition monitoring as the fourth monitoring category.

Built by Peter Higgins / Rogue Wave Audio
with the HUF AI Collective: Claude (Anthropic), ChatGPT (OpenAI),
Copilot (Microsoft), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI)

March 2026

post_publish_checklist.2_check_readme: README.md renders properly as the repo landing page
post_publish_checklist.3_check_license: MIT license displays in the repo sidebar
post_publish_checklist.4_check_structure: All 7 top-level directories visible: context-books, drafts, huf-cls, huf-gov, math-books, process, reference
post_publish_checklist.5_check_analyzer: The spectrum analyzer JSX/HTML files are accessible
post_publish_checklist.6_check_json: Machine-readable JSONs render properly in GitHub's viewer
post_publish_checklist.7_update_letter: Update the Peterson letter with the live GitHub URL if it contains a placeholder
post_publish_checklist.8_breathe: You filed. The timestamp exists. Bell, not Gray.

do_not_include: The EMBER raw CSV data files (these are CC BY 4.0 but large — the analyzed JSON summaries are already in the repo)
Any files from the Claude Cowork session folder that aren't in the HUF-repo structure
The Peterson letter (this goes in RWA, not HUF)
warnings.sensitive_files_check: Review process/governance-docs/ before publishing. The Personal_Plan and Deployment_Strategy contain strategic information. Peter should confirm these are OK to publish.
warnings.toronto_data_note: The Toronto case study includes .geojson and .csv files from the City of Toronto Open Data portal. These are public domain. OK to include.
